program_name: Sustainable Chemistry and Technologies for Circular Economy
degree_class: LM-71 R - Sciences and Technologies of Industrial Chemistry

academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a three-year degree or equivalent foreign qualification. They must either hold one of the specified chemistry degree classes or prove the required 40 ECTS across the listed scientific disciplinary sectors.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Degree or university diploma of three years' duration, or a foreign equivalent deemed suitable for admission purposes.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Applicants must either hold a degree in class L-27 Chemical Sciences and Technologies ex DM 270/04 or class L-21 Chemical Sciences and Technologies ex DM 509/99, or have acquired at least 40 ECTS in the listed Scientific Disciplinary Sectors: 12 ECTS in CHIM/01, CHIM/02, CHIM/03, CHIM/04, CHIM/05, CHIM/06, CHIM/07, GEO/06; and 28 ECTS in MAT/01-09, FIS/01-03, BIO/01, BIO/10, GEO/04, GEO/07, GEO/09, CHIM/11, CHIM/12, ING-IND/35. For highly-qualified graduates with proven knowledge and competencies from previous training programmes not completely coherent with the admission requirements, a different entry route and/or specific admission tests could apply.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: No universal programme-specific GPA threshold is published on the reviewed official pages. Applicants using international qualifications should also verify any country-based entry-title and GPA rules on the University of Padua application platform.

language_requirements.required_level: English B2 (CEFR) or equivalent, with reading, comprehension, and listening explicitly mentioned on the programme pages.
accepted_proofs: IELTS Academic or General Training: minimum score 6.0.
Cambridge English B2 First: minimum score 173.
Trinity College London Integrated Skills in English ISE II.
Oxford Test of English: minimum score 126.
GATEHOUSE ESOL International Classic B2: GA Level 1 Certificate.
Pearson Test of English General: PTE General 3.
Pearson English International Certificate: Level 3 Upper Intermediate.
Pearson Test of English Academic: minimum score 65.
exemption_rules: Applicants are exempt if they obtained the entry qualification by completing their previous education entirely in English.
Applicants are exempt if they are native English speakers from the countries listed by the University of Padua.

application_deadline_eu.notes: The official apply.unipd.it admissions page shows Call Three as 2 June 2026 - 2 August 2026 and states that it is only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy. The official general programme page currently shows a likely typo with the start date written as 2 June 2025.

application_deadline_non_eu.call_three: Not available for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy [uncertain]
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The official apply.unipd.it admissions page shows Call Three only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy. The official general programme page currently shows a likely typo with the Call Three start date written as 2 June 2025 instead of 2 June 2026.

required_documents.notes: Upload a colour scanned PDF. If needed, provide an English or Italian translation. No legalised documents are required at application stage.

required_documents.document_name: Academic qualification or provisional enrolment certificate
required_documents.notes: If the final qualification has not yet been awarded, an enrolment or provisional certificate is accepted.
EU citizens and non-EU citizens residing in Italy may use an ID card; other applicants normally provide a passport.
Required only for non-EU applicants residing in Italy.

required_documents.document_name: English language certificate or other accepted proof
required_documents.notes: Required unless the applicant qualifies for an official exemption.
The University asks applicants to check the entry requirements of the chosen programme for any additional required document.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's diploma translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: Required after admission to complete enrolment; a CIMEA Statement of Verification can be used as an alternative in the cases stated by the University.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of Records translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: The transcript must be issued by the competent academic authority and show passed exams, grades, and programme description.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment and study visa D - enrolment
required_documents.notes: Non-EU citizens residing outside Italy must pre-enrol through Universitaly no later than 15 July and obtain a study visa.

required_documents.document_name: Residence permit or residence-permit application receipt
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU applicants who are in Italy at enrolment stage.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Programme-specific admission test may apply in exceptional cases
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Highly-qualified graduates whose previous training is not completely coherent with the admission requirements.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: The department page states that for highly-qualified graduates with proven knowledge and competencies resulting from previous training programmes that are not completely coherent with the admission requirements, a different entry route and/or specific admission tests could apply. No standard entry exam for all applicants is published.

source_quotes.quote: Access Open access with admission requirements
hold a degree in one of the following degree classes: L-27 Chemical Sciences and Technologies ex DM 270/04 or L-21 Chemical Sciences and Technologies ex DM 509/99 or have acquired at least 40 ECTS
have a B2 English level (reading and comprehension and listening).
a different entry route and/or specific admission tests could apply.
Call Three: 2 June 2026 – 2 August 2026 (only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy)
Call Three: 2 June 2025 – 2 August 2026 (only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy)

uncertainty_notes: The official general programme page for 2026/2027 currently shows a likely typo in the Call Three start date ('2 June 2025'), while the official apply.unipd.it admissions page shows '2 June 2026'. The application-deadline fields are therefore marked uncertain and reported using the admissions-platform date.
No separate programme-specific PDF admission notice was found beyond the official pages reviewed on 2026-06-04.
